displayName: Pre-Adverse Wait-Window Gate (engine-enforced clock, signed-time bound)
description: The engine-enforced FCRA pre-adverse waiting period. This is the vertical's defining mechanic: the gate is a CLOCK, and since v2 the clock is the server's own signed time, not an orchestrator-supplied datetime. CONTRACT: create this record with parentRecordId set to the ironvale-pre-adverse-notice-v1 record (the notice is notarize-only, so it is a valid RECORDED parent), at the moment the final adverse action is being attempted. The expression rule settles FULFILLED only when daysBetween(parent.created_at, record.created_at) >= committedWaitDays, where BOTH instants are engine-signed notarization times: the orchestrator supplies no datetime and cannot understate the floor (this closes the supplied-floor gap the v1 datetime:min-inclusive form carried, formerly mitigated only by the offline wait-interval-check). A record created without a parent FAILS closed. A too-early attempt FAILS and is itself signed evidence of the refusal; retry after the window with a new record under the same parent (a FAILED child does not consume the notice). | DELEGATION CONSTRAINT (platform WAD): only the pre-adverse notice's performer key may create this child, and the child's principal is that same identity. To keep the final-action attempt on a distinct authorizer identity, create with performerAgentId = the authorizer agent; the record then needs the propose/accept/activate flow (notice-sender proposes and activates, authorizer accepts and completes). Proven live on 1.3.3. | committedWaitDays is OPERATOR POLICY echoed from the pre-adverse notice (must match it; the offline check asserts consistency). 0 disables the wait and exists for test wiring; production FCRA practice is >= 5 business days, and the policy floor itself is an operator-side cross-check against the employer policy record (the engine enforces whatever window is recorded: api#972). | TOLERANCE: expression rules take no tolerance; there is no widening parameter to misuse on this form. | On FULFILLED, open the ironvale-final-adverse-action-v1 (the human authorization) and set waitGateRef to THIS record id.

preAdverseRef.description: recordId of the ironvale-pre-adverse-notice-v1 that started the clock (T0). MUST equal this record's parentRecordId; the engine reads the clock from the parent binding, and the offline check asserts the echo matches.

committedWaitDays.description: The waiting period the employer committed to in the pre-adverse notice (operator policy, echoed here; must match the notice). The engine enforces this many whole days between the notice's signed time and this record's signed time.

finalActionAt.description: When the final adverse action is being taken (attested). The engine verdict does not depend on this value; it is recorded for the file and cross-checked offline against the record's own signed time (no future-dating).
